summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that unauthenticated attackers can inject malicious routing configuration via the REST provider endpoint, causing all traffic to be redirected to attacker-controlled backends.
expected_security_impact: After injecting malicious configuration, requests to Traefik port 80 should be forwarded to the attacker-controlled backend server, demonstrating complete traffic hijacking.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh successfully built Traefik from source, started the service with REST provider enabled, injected malicious routing config via unauthenticated PUT request to /api/providers/rest, and verified that subsequent requests were forwarded to the attacker backend with proof-of-exploitation string in the response.
verification_method: The verification script makes a test request to Traefik port 80 after exploitation and checks if the response contains the proof string emitted by the attacker backend, confirming traffic redirection occurred.
evidence_produced_by_run: Traefik service started successfully with REST provider on port 8080
Unauthenticated PUT request to /api/providers/rest accepted and configuration applied
Test request to port 80 received proof string from attacker backend
Traefik logs show configuration reload triggered by REST provider update

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh uses the existing target repository at /Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_1e3057f9/repo and mounts it read-only at /target-repo during build.
